Class ProjectService

java.lang.Object
de.hallerweb.enterprise.prioritize.service.project.ProjectService
@Service @Transactional public class ProjectService extends Object
Manages projects and their team, resources and documents. Access is membership-based rather than role-based: the manager or any member may read a project, while only the manager may modify it or manage its team. Each project owns a Blackboard that is created together with the project.
Author:
peter haller

    static final record 
    ProjectService.ProjectData
    Immutable set of editable project fields, decoupling the service from HTTP DTOs.

  • Constructor Details

    • ProjectService

      public ProjectService()

  • Method Details

    • createProject

      public Project createProject(ProjectService.ProjectData data, PUser creator)
      Creates a new project with an empty blackboard. The creator becomes the manager and is added as the first member.

      A project has no owning container to authorize against (unlike resources/documents, which are created into a department or group), so creation is gated by a type-level Action.CREATE permission on Project — a PermissionRecord with objectId == 0. Admins are allowed implicitly. Read/update/delete of an existing project stay membership-based.

      Parameters:
      data - the project's initial field values
      creator - the authenticated user creating the project
      Returns:
      the persisted project
      Throws:
      org.springframework.security.access.AccessDeniedException - if the creator may not create projects

    • getProject

      @Transactional(readOnly=true) public Project getProject(Long projectId, PUser user)
      Returns the project if the user is its manager or a member.
      Parameters:
      projectId - the project id
      user - the requesting user
      Returns:
      the project

    • getMyProjects

      @Transactional(readOnly=true) public List<Project> getMyProjects(PUser user)
      Returns all projects the user manages or participates in.
      Parameters:
      user - the requesting user
      Returns:
      the user's projects (possibly empty)

    • updateProject

      public Project updateProject(Long projectId, ProjectService.ProjectData data, PUser user)
      Updates the editable fields of a project. Manager only.
      Parameters:
      projectId - the project id
      data - the new field values
      user - the requesting user
      Returns:
      the updated project

    • deleteProject

      public void deleteProject(Long projectId, PUser user)
      Deletes a project (and, by cascade, its blackboard and tasks). Manager only.

      Entities that satellite packages attach to a project (task schedules, …) are not reachable from Project and would keep their foreign key pointing at the vanishing row, so a ProjectDeletionEvent is published first and its listeners clean up synchronously, inside this transaction.

      Parameters:
      projectId - the project id
      user - the requesting user

    • addMember

      public Project addMember(Long projectId, Long userId, PUser user)

      public Project removeMember(Long projectId, Long userId, PUser user)

      public Project addResource(Long projectId, Long resourceId, PUser user)

      public Project removeResource(Long projectId, Long resourceId, PUser user)

      public Project addDocument(Long projectId, Long documentInfoId, PUser user)

    • removeDocument

      public Project removeDocument(Long projectId, Long documentInfoId, PUser user)

    • transferManager

      public Project transferManager(Long projectId, Long newManagerId, PUser actor)
      Hands the project over to another of its members. Allowed for the acting manager and for an administrator — the latter so a project whose manager has left the company does not stay unmanageable forever, since the manager is otherwise fixed at creation time.

      The new manager must already be a member: a hand-over changes who is in charge, never who is on the team, and an administrator who wants the project for themselves joins it first (which addMember(Long, Long, PUser) lets them do). The outgoing manager stays a member and can afterwards be removed normally — which removeMember(Long, Long, PUser)'s guard prevents while they are still in charge.

      Parameters:
      projectId - the project id
      newManagerId - the id of the member taking over
      actor - the requesting user (current manager or an administrator)
      Returns:
      the updated project
      Throws:
      org.springframework.security.access.AccessDeniedException - if the actor is neither the manager nor an administrator
      IllegalArgumentException - if the designated user is not a member of the project

    • findOrThrow

      public Project findOrThrow(Long projectId)
      Loads a project or throws if it does not exist.
      Parameters:
      projectId - the project id
      Returns:
      the managed project
      Throws:
      NoSuchElementException - if no such project exists

    • requireMemberOrManager

      public void requireMemberOrManager(Project project, PUser user)
      Ensures the user is the manager or a member of the project.
      Throws:
      org.springframework.security.access.AccessDeniedException - if the user is neither manager nor member

    • requireManager

      public void requireManager(Project project, PUser user)
      Ensures the user is the manager of the project.
      Throws:
      org.springframework.security.access.AccessDeniedException - if the user is not the manager

    • requireManagerOrAdmin

      public void requireManagerOrAdmin(Project project, PUser user)
      Ensures the user is the manager of the project, or an administrator.

      Deliberately narrower than it looks: only the team management and the hand-over accept an administrator. Editing or deleting a project stays with its manager alone, so that admin rights do not quietly turn into blanket access to every project's content — an administrator who needs that takes the project over first, which is a visible, logged act.

      Throws:
      org.springframework.security.access.AccessDeniedException - if the user is neither the manager nor an administrator
